Document [17] (Title: Airway for Laser Surgery -- Equipment -- Commonly Used Lasers) Carbon dioxide (CO2) lasers are historically the most commonly used lasers in otolaryngologic surgery. [11] Use a visible-light aiming beam and ensure this aiming beam is accurate to the invisible laser before beginning the operation. Eyes (of both the patient and the operating room personnel) are especially vulnerable to the CO 2 laser, though simple, clear glass or plastic is sufficient for protection.
